mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving real activity and operating leverage for listed firms, raising expected earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing investors to cut earnings expectations and reduce equity exposure, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate signals tighter real financial conditions and higher discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-and-rate tightening trigger does not produce lower 20-day forward returns than either single-series condition alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum for consumer-facing firms, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above its prior 12-month average growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if triggered retail sales accelerations have a 20-day forward hit rate below 50% or if their mean forward return is not greater than the full-sample mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction-related income, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10 percent over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if 6-month housing-start declines larger than 10 percent are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth with a stable or falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-market expansion and resilient aggregate income, supporting revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE does not rise over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-growth and non-rising-unemployment condition fails to outperform unconditional 5-day S&P 500 returns after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth signals a broad expansion in aggregate demand and corporate cash-flow capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after the latest available GDP release.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is greater than 0 percent
failure_condition: Reject if positive year-over-year GDPC1 growth is not associated with above-median 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising while industrial production is falling, stagflation pressure raises discount-rate concerns and lowers real earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and INDPRO 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if this inflation-up and production-down interaction has no negative incremental effect on 20-day S&P 500 returns relative to CPIAUCSL and INDPRO single-factor signals.
